question: A sum of money is borrowed and paid back in two equal annual instalments of ₹882 each, allowing 5% compound interest. What was the sum borrowed?

The compound interest on a sum of money for two years is ₹3328 and the simple interest on the same sum for the same period is ₹3200. What will be the difference between compound interest and simple interest for three years?
The difference between the simple interest on a certain amount at 20% per annum for 1.5 years and compound interest at the same rate compounded every six months is ₹155. Find the principal amount.
In how many years will a sum of ₹18000 at 20% per annum compounded half-yearly become ₹23958?
A sum of money doubles itself in 5 years. In how many years will it become four times itself if interest is compounded?
What annual payment will discharge a debt of ₹808 due in 2 years at 2% per annum, if equal payments are made at the end of each year?

An amount of ₹12820 due 3 years hence is fully repaid in three annual instalments starting after 1 year. The first instalment is half the second and the second is two-thirds of the third. If the interest rate is 10% per annum, find the first instalment.

A sum is divided between A and B in the ratio 1 : 2. A purchases a car that depreciates at 14 2/7% per annum while B deposits his amount in a bank paying 20% per annum compounded annually. Approximately by what percentage will the total wealth increase after two years?

Michael Bolton has $90,000. He buys a car, laptop and flat for $15,000, $13,000 and $35,000 respectively and deposits the remaining amount at 15% compound interest. After 2 years, he sells all assets at 80% of their original prices and closes the bank account. What is the approximate percentage change in his total assets?

Mohit Anand borrows a sum from a bank at 10% compound interest. The debt is discharged by two equal annual payments of ₹1000 each at the end of the first and second years. Approximately what amount was borrowed?

Amit borrowed ₹168000 at 10% per annum compounded annually. He repaid the loan in two equal annual instalments. The first instalment paid off the first year's interest and part of the principal, while the second instalment cleared the balance. What was the value of each instalment?

Mr. Vinay invested ₹2025 in a crypto trading app. The investment appreciated for 2 years at 20% compound growth. The final amount equals the amount obtained after depreciating ₹4000 for 3 years at a constant compound rate. Find the difference between the appreciation rate and the depreciation rate.

Kaalia borrowed money at 15% simple interest for 2 years. He then lent half of it to Mallya at 16% compounded half-yearly and the other half to Jaalia at 15% compounded annually, both for 2 years. Approximately what percentage profit on the original amount did Kaalia earn?

Mr. Mehra has two sons aged 15 and 12 years. He divides ₹20 lakh into two parts and invests them at simple interest so that each son receives ₹25 lakh on reaching the age of 21. What annual rate of simple interest should the investments yield?

Mr. Suratkal divides ₹20 lakh between his two sons aged 15 and 12 years so that both receive the same amount at age 21. If the investments earn 12% compounded annually, approximately how much should be invested for the younger son?

You borrow the same amount at 12% simple interest and at 10% simple interest for the same period. The first loan is repaid six months early while the second is repaid on schedule. In both cases the amount repaid is ₹92625. Determine the principal and the time period.

The population growth rate of a city follows an increasing AP with first-year rate 5% and common difference 5%, while migration follows an increasing GP with first term 1% and common ratio 2. If the population on 31 December 2010 is one million, in which year will the city witness its first fall in population?

A certain sum of money was lent under the following simple interest scheme: 8% per annum for the initial 2 years, 9.5% per annum for the next 4 years, 11% per annum for the next 2 years and 12% per annum thereafter. Find the amount to which a sum of ₹9000 taken for 12 years will grow.
The simple interest on a certain sum of money for three years at 8% per annum is half the compound interest on ₹4000 for two years at 10% per annum. Find the sum placed at simple interest.
Ranjeet makes a deposit of ₹50,000 in a bank for a period of 2.5 years. If the rate of interest is 12% per annum compounded half-yearly, find the maturity value of the deposit.

A certain amount grows at an annual interest rate of 12%, compounded monthly. Which of the following equations can be used to find the number of years y required for the investment to increase by a factor of 64?

A man invests a total of ₹17 lakh. Part of it is invested at 6%, and the remainder is invested in two banks in the ratio 3:1 at 5% and 4% respectively. If the total annual interest earned is ₹92000, find the amount invested at 6%.

topic: Simple Interest with Different Starting Times
question: Abhijit invested ₹20000 at 6% simple interest, while Amartya invested ₹18000 at 10% simple interest five years later. After how many years from Abhijit's investment will Amartya's accumulated amount exceed Abhijit's accumulated amount?
